commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: And as, in those times, vengeance came from God upon the Egyptians who were subjecting Israel to unjust punishment, so is it now, the Lord truly declaring, "And shall not God avenge His own elect, which cry day and night unto Him? I tell you, that He will avenge them speedily."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
He show us that it is God the judge whom we must importune with prayer, and not Himself, if He is not Himself the judge. But He added, that "God would avenge His own elect.".
He is commanding that those about to confess be the object of solicitude; and He is soothing their sufferings when He asserts that God will avenge His own elect.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
